channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Understanding ACL Injuries: Causes, Recovery, and Long-Term Impact
item.description: Anterior cruciate ligament (ACL) injuries are among the most feared by athletes, but they affect people of all ages, not just professionals. The ACL connects the thigh and shin bones and plays a criti...

item.title: Government announces extra $53 billion for Defence over the next decade to reach 3 per cent of GDP
item.description: The Australian Government will inject an additional $53 billion into Defence spending over the next ten years, as part of an updated National Defence Strategy to be released by Defence Minister Richar...

item.title: Two Men Killed in Datu Paglas Ambush Amid Rising Violence in Maguindanao del Sur
item.description: In a continuing pattern of violence in Maguindanao del Sur, two men riding a motorcycle were ambushed and killed on April 14, 2026, in Barangay Damawato, Datu Paglas. One of the victims, Ibrahim Matuk...

item.title: Alberta Takes Independent Policy Actions Diverging from Federal Standards
item.description: Over the past few years, Alberta has increasingly challenged federal authority by implementing provincial policies that differ from Ottawa’s standards. Notable actions include tightening eligibility f...

item.title: Pakistani Delegation Led by CDF Munir Visits Tehran to Support US-Iran Mediation
item.description: Chief of Defence Forces and Army Chief Field Marshal Asim Munir reached Tehran on Wednesday along with Interior Minister Mohsin Naqvi and a formal delegation to continue Pakistan's role in mediation b...

item.title: AA and BSM driving schools to refund 80,000 learners and pay £4.2m fine over hidden booking fees
item.description: The Competition and Markets Authority (CMA) has ordered AA Driving School and BSM Driving School, both owned by the AA, to refund more than 80,000 learner drivers and pay a total fine of £4.2 million ...

item.title: Historic and Current Fuel Conservation Measures in Australia
item.description: The Australian government has launched a new campaign urging citizens to reduce fuel consumption amid a global oil crisis. This follows Prime Minister Anthony Albanese’s visit to Singapore, which supp...

item.title: Opposition INDIA Bloc Resolves to Oppose Centre's Delimitation Bill in Parliament
item.description: NEW DELHI: Congress president Mallikarjun Kharge on Wednesday announced that opposition parties united under the INDIA bloc have resolved to strongly oppose the Centre's proposed delimitation bill in ...

item.title: China expands military support in Somalia amid Taiwan-Somaliland diplomatic tensions
item.description: China has recently pledged to increase its military support to Somalia in the fight against al-Shabaab militants, providing training, equipment, and closer security cooperation with Mogadishu. This is...

item.title: Diplomatic Efforts Underway to Extend U.S.-Iran Ceasefire Amid Rising Tensions
item.description: Mediators are actively seeking to extend the fragile ceasefire between the United States and Iran, which is set to expire on April 22, 2026. Despite ongoing diplomatic efforts, tensions remain high as...

item.title: MMF Urges Renewed Efforts to Find Remaining Chibok Girls as Decade Passes
item.description: As Nigeria dey mark twelve years since 276 schoolgirls vanish from Chibok, the Murtala Muhammed Foundation (MMF) don call for serious accountability. According to MMF report, 89 girls still dey missin...

item.title: Canadian Coast Guard to Test Israeli-Made Drone for Arctic Surveillance and Defence
item.description: The Canadian Coast Guard is preparing to conduct test flights this summer in the Arctic using an Israeli-made Hermes 900 StarLiner drone. The drone, originally purchased by Transport Canada in 2023 fo...

item.title: President Ruto Sends Encouraging Message to Harambee Starlets Before Match with Australia
item.description: President William Ruto has publicly shown his support for the Harambee Starlets, Kenya's national women's football team, ahead of their upcoming match against Australia. In a video shared by Statehous...

item.title: X's Mass Bot Removal Disrupts Users' Private Accounts for Curating Adult Content
item.description: X (formerly Twitter) has intensified its campaign against automated accounts, suspending thousands of bots at a rate of 208 per minute as announced by head of product Nikita Bier. While the effort aim...

item.title: Oil prices edge higher as Hormuz transit stays restricted despite diplomatic efforts
item.description: Oil prices climbed over 1 per cent as continued disruptions through the Strait of Hormuz overshadowed growing optimism about renewed U.S.-Iran negotiations. Forty-five days after Iran’s Revolutionary ...

item.title: Ena Coach Adjusts Fares After EPRA Fuel Price Review
item.description: Ena Coach, one of Kenya’s leading long-distance bus operators, has announced an immediate increase in fares for several key routes across the country. The company attributed the decision to recent fue...

item.title: Gunmen target Tamontaka 4 barangay hall in Cotabato City, residents unharmed
item.description: Early on Tuesday, April 14, 2026, gunmen fired assault rifles at the newly-renovated barangay hall of Tamontaka 4 in Cotabato City, causing damage to the rear wall and glass panes of the building. The...

item.title: Orengo Demands Transparency from EPRA on Fuel Price Increases
item.description: Siaya Governor James Orengo has publicly criticised the Energy and Petroleum Regulatory Authority (EPRA) over the recent fuel price hike, describing the situation as unfair and misleading to Kenyan ta...

item.title: Government urges internal auditors to maintain independence and strengthen accountability
item.description: Internal auditors from public institutions across Tanzania and Zanzibar have been urged to maintain independence and enhance efficiency in their work, ensuring public funds and resources are properly ...

item.title: Jack Hughes Loses Teeth but Scores Winning Goal at Olympic Hockey Final
item.description: During the 2026 Winter Olympics hockey final, Jack Hughes, representing the USA, suffered a painful blow to his mouth from Canadian player Sam Bennet, which led to him losing his front teeth. Even tho...
